pullRequest.title: Replace nose with pytest
pullRequest.body:
Replace nose with pytest

1. nose testing library has been dead for a while
https://nose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/#note-to-users
and distros have been removing downstream packages
https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Changes/DeprecateNose

    Let’s switch to pytest, which is the most popular these days.

2. Furthermore `setup.py test` subcommand has been removed in setuptools 72.0.0:
https://www.github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/931
and it is not recommended by pytest:
https://docs.pytest.org/en/7.1.x/explanation/goodpractices.html#do-not-run-via-setuptools

    Let’s call pytest directly.

3. `test_suite` field in `setup.py` was meant to preclude the following tests from running

        brushiconeditor (unittest.loader._FailedTest.brushiconeditor) ... ERROR
        drawwindow (unittest.loader._FailedTest.drawwindow) ... ERROR

    but using the equivalent `pytest.testpaths` option is not necessary since pytest requires
    files to be named according to convention, which those gui modules do not follow.

4. `setup.py test` called `build_ext --inplace`, we need to enable it ourselves.
    In-place builds are ugly but `build` command already adds stuff into the source tree
    so this should be fine until we switch to Meson.

5. Some tests print performance metrics to `stderr` so we will use `pytest -rP`
   to also print captured stderr for passed tests. In the future, we will replace
   that with user properties:
   https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/reference/reference.html#record-property

Fixes: https://github.com/mypaint/mypaint/issues/1191

pullRequest.title: Fix SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\*'
pullRequest.body:
These made doctests slighly more noisy.

Fixes: https://github.com/mypaint/mypaint/issues/1312
